title: Something horrible
wikitext:
[]: Something horrible...             Quote Post by Iblis 19th Angel » Thu Jul 21, 2005 4:48 am Remember how in the old days, when an anime show was sent across the Pacific, instead of keeping the original Japanese opening theme song intact or replacing it with a completely differenty song altogether like they do today, they'd rewrite the theme song with english lyrics? Tetsuwan Atom/Astroboy's theme is a pretty good example of this. Well, I wondered what it would be like if that had been the case with our favourite giant-cyborg-mindfuck series, so I wrote my own english lyrics. And, being the sadistic bastard I am, I'm going to share them with you. Be thankful I'm only posting the TV-size version. There's more but it's even worse. And now, on to the fun... The cruel angel heralds the beginning of the end, Young boy rise to the heavens like a legend... *(Short instrumental)* Picture a world, not unlike your own, Where people believe that they're safe in their home, But just outside their door, A supernatural horror, Is out to destroy the human race. Picture a child, not unlike yourself, Who goes through life not caring about himself, Just spending his days, Trying to shut away, All the things that he cannot face. Earth is in crisis, 3rd impact's approaching, As Angels descend from the sky. If humankind's to survive what's encroaching, Then they're gonna have to rely, On- Giant robots with angels trapped inside of them, Teenaged pilots & an evil organization, Doing battle with big, black monsters from beyond, Mankind's last hope lies in the Evangelions. In his Eva, Shinji Ikari battles on, Against the Angels & his own inner demons. Catchy, no?

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Thu Jul 21, 2005 4:54 am Horrible, indeed.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Thu Jul 21, 2005 6:09 pm wow... you must have WAY too much free time on your hands

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Thu Jul 21, 2005 7:17 pm S^3, ever heard of fanfiction?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 21, 2005 7:35 pm ...ever heard of the Fan-Geeks Commentary Project? It deserves much more of my "free time" than I currently give it.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Jul 21, 2005 7:36 pm I think I have some fan fics to finish one of these days, when watching bootleges cartoons dosn't take up so much time . . . . .

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Thu Jul 21, 2005 8:11 pm Reichu wrote: ...ever heard of the Fan-Geeks Commentary Project? It deserves much more of my "free time" than I currently give it.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Jul 21, 2005 8:20 pm Ryo"][quote="Reichu wrote: On the topic of fanfiction: Did I mention that I read "Higher Learning" in one sitting last mont? Go me.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Thu Jul 21, 2005 8:25 pm Ghu, no. From what I've heard it's very over-rated. I'm not into the "Eva continued" type of fanfictions, either. In fact, I hate them. I hate them more than any kind of fanfiction on this god-forsaken planet. Heck, I'd rather read Shinji/Rei WAFF!

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jul 21, 2005 9:19 pm Ryo wrote: On topic of that, how's the progress going? When I joined AN the Ep. 4 Commentary was pretty much history, and Ep. 5 is still looming in the distance. Sadamoto will finish his Eva manga and probably make a sequal or two before we finally get around to the mushy parts in EoE!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 21, 2005 9:20 pm By "Eva continued", do you mean something that just stretches the whole saga out and provides more, and more, and more of the same? If so... I hate them, too. As for episode #05... er... :signs off forums:

[]:             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Thu Jul 21, 2005 10:41 pm sadly, adv beat you to it. the original region 1 release actually had a translation that allowed you to sing it in time with the music. but just like many books of foreign songs translated into english, they took liberties with the lyrics and drove the original phrasing and meaning into the ground. thank god that went away in platinum. but at least you've whooped them in the sheer cheesiness factor. :p

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Fri Jul 22, 2005 5:30 am Reichu wrote: By "Eva continued", do you mean something that just stretches the whole saga out and provides more, and more, and more of the same? If so... I hate them, too.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Fri Jul 22, 2005 9:00 am oops...fan fiction...lol eva-r was...strange, at best I prefer the fan-fics that try to fit into the episode timeline, because the others are usually a bit too predictable (anything post 3I with Asuka and Shinji is never a surprise, unless it is a darkfic)

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Fri Jul 22, 2005 9:33 am sadsadshinji wrote: (anything post 3I with Asuka and Shinji is never a surprise, unless it is a darkfic)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jul 22, 2005 3:03 pm Ryo wrote: That's exactly what I meant. New Angels, new Evas and new Children for NO FRIGGING REASON AT ALL!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Fri Jul 22, 2005 5:41 pm ... I hate you. :evil:

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 22, 2005 7:24 pm In GOS2, anyway, Asuka uses Kensuke in attempts to make Shinji jealous. And, if she doesn't end up with Shinji at the end, she takes Kensuke as her consolation prize. Oh well, it's not like there's any other male character in her age group that's available for these kinds of scenarios. Toji and Hikari are an item, and Kaworu is gay (but he makes an exception for Rei), so if she doesn't get Baka-Shinji, there's only one option left.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Fri Jul 22, 2005 7:31 pm Sigh. I've always bothered why many fanfic authors does like that - I've read countless where Shinji end up with Asuka, Toji get loovey-doovey with Hikari and Kensuke and Rei are dating. How can you support a pairing like Kensuke/Asuka or Kensuke/Rei? It goes far beyond my mind. :?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 22, 2005 7:40 pm Yeah, honestly. Kensuke needs some nice geeky chick... I wonder if that Mayumi character would fit the bill?
summary: []: Something horrible...             Quote Post by Iblis 19th Angel » Thu Jul 21, 2005 4:48 am Remember how in the old days, when an anime show was sent across the Pacific, instead of keeping the original Japanese opening theme song intact or replacing it with a completely differenty song altogether like they do today, they'd rewrite the theme song with english lyrics? Tetsuwan Atom/Astroboy's theme is a pretty good example of this
